note: Focuses on the particularity of the 'world' (often interpreted as all kinds of people, not every individual) and the sovereignty of God's love as the cause of salvation.
Emphasizes the 'objectivity' of the Gospel promise—that the gift is truly offered to all through the Word, and faith is the hand that receives it.
Highlights the necessity of responding to God's love with a 'faith working through love' (Gal 5:6), emphasizing the sacramental context of rebirth mentioned earlier in John 3.
Central text for 'Sola Fide'. Justification is a forensic declaration—God declaring the sinner righteous because of Christ's work, received by faith alone.
Focuses on the 'Logos' as the basis for theosis (divinization). God became man so that man might become like God. The Word is the archetype of all creation.
Emphasis on faith as an active, living assurance that transforms the believer's life, leading toward Christian perfection and holiness.
